segments.text: Concerning Moab, this is what the LORD of Hosts, the God of Israel, says:
segments.speaker: The LORD of hosts

segments.text: Woe to Nebo, for it will be devastated. Kiriathaim will be captured and disgraced; the fortress will be shattered and dismantled.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
There is no longer praise for Moab; in Heshbon they devise evil against her:
‘Come, let us cut her off from nationhood.’
You too, O people of Madmen,  will be silenced; the sword will pursue you.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: A voice cries out from Horonaim:‘Devastation and great destruction!’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Moab will be shattered; her little ones will cry out.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
For on the ascent to Luhiththey weep bitterly as they go, and on the descent to Horonaimcries of distress resound
over the destruction:
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: ‘Flee! Run for your lives! Become like a juniper in the desert. ’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Because you trust in your works and treasures, you too will be captured, and Chemosh will go into exilewith his priests and officials.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
The destroyer will move against every city, and not one town will escape. The valley will also be ruined, and the high plain will be destroyed, 
as the LORD has said.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Put salt on Moab, for she will be laid waste;  her cities will become desolate, with no one to dwell in them.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Cursed is the one who is remissin doing the work of the LORD, and cursed is he who withholdshis sword from bloodshed.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Moab has been at ease from youth, settled like wine on its dregs; he has not been poured from vessel to vesselor gone into exile. So his flavor has remained the same, and his aroma is unchanged.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Therefore behold, the days are coming, declares the LORD, when I will send to him wanderers, who will pour him out. They will empty his vessels and shatter his jars.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
Then Moab will be ashamed of Chemosh, just as the house of Israel was ashamed
when they trusted in Bethel.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: How can you say, ‘We are warriors, mighty men ready for battle’?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Moab has been destroyed and its towns have been invaded; the best of its young menhave gone down in the slaughter, declares the King, whose name is the LORD of Hosts.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Moab’s calamity is at hand, and his affliction is rushing swiftly.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Mourn for him, all you who surround him, everyone who knows his name; tell how the mighty scepter is shattered—the glorious staff!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Come down from your glory; sit on parched ground, O daughter dwelling in Dibon, for the destroyer of Moab has come against you; he has destroyed your fortresses.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: St and by the road and watch, O dweller of Aroer! Ask the man fleeing or the woman escaping,‘What has happened?’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Moab is put to shame, for it has been shattered. Wail and cry out! Declare by the Arnonthat Moab is destroyed.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Judgment has come upon the high plain—upon Holon, Jahzah,  and Mephaath,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: upon Dibon, Nebo, and Beth-diblathaim,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: upon Kiriathaim, Beth-gamul, and Beth-meon,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: upon Kerioth, Bozrah, and all the towns of Moab, those far and near.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The horn of Moab has been cut off, and his arm is broken,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: declares the LORD.
Make him drunk, because he has magnified himself against the LORD; so Moab will wallow in his own vomit, and he will also become a laughingstock.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Was not Israel your object of ridicule? Was he ever found among thieves? For whenever you speak of himyou shake your head.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Abandon the towns and settle among the rocks, O dwellers of Moab! Be like a dovethat nests at the mouth of a cave.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
We have heard of Moab’s pomposity, his exceeding pride and conceit, 
his proud arrogance and haughtiness of heart.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I know his insolence,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: declares the LORD,

but it is futile. 
His boasting is as empty as his deeds.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
Therefore I will wail for Moab; I will cry out for all of Moab; 
I will moan for the men of Kir-heres.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I will weep for you, O vine of Sibmah, more than I weep for Jazer. Your tendrils have extended to the sea;  they reach even to Jazer.  The destroyer has descendedon your summer fruit and grape harvest.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
Joy and gladness are removed from the orchard and from the fields of Moab. I have stopped the flow of wine from the presses; no one treads them with shouts of joy; 
their shouts are not for joy.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: There is a cry from Heshbon to Elealeh; they raise their voices to Jahaz, from Zoar to Horonaim and Eglath-shelishiyah; for even the waters of Nimrim have dried up.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: In Moab, declares the LORD, I will bring an endto those who make offerings on the high places and burn incense to their gods.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
Therefore My heart laments like a flute for Moab; it laments like a flute for the men of Kir-heres, 
because the wealth they acquired has perished.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: For every head is shaved and every beard is clipped; on every hand is a gash, and around every waist is sackcloth.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: On all the rooftops of Moab and in the public squares, everyone is mourning; for I have shattered Moab like an unwanted jar,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: declares the LORD.
How shattered it is! How they wail! How Moab has turned his back in shame! Moab has become an object of ridicule and horrorto all those around him.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For this is what the LORD says:
Behold, an eagle swoops down and spreads his wings against Moab.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Kirioth has been taken, and the strongholds seized. In that day the heart of Moab’s warriorswill be like the heart of a woman in labor.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Moab will be destroyed as a nationbecause he vaunted himself against the LORD.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Terror and pit and snare await you, O dweller of Moab,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: declares the LORD.
Whoever flees the panicwill fall into the pit, and whoever climbs from the pitwill be caught in the snare. For I will bring upon Moabthe year of their punishment,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: declares the LORD.

Those who flee will st and helpless in Heshbon’s shadow, because fire has gone forth from Heshbon
and a flame from within Sihon. It devours the foreheads of Moab and the skulls of the sons of tumult.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Woe to you, O Moab! The people of Chemosh have perished; for your sons have been taken into exile and your daughters have gone into captivity.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Yet in the latter days I will restore Moab from captivity,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
declares the LORD.
Here ends the judgment on Moab.
